title: flaky, fiery british fish paella
description: a crowd-pleasing british dish featuring flaky fish, a spicy kick from aekjeot, and the crunch of katsuramuki-cut vegetables, all cooked to a golden, yeasty crisp in an iron teppan.

ingredients.name: smoked haddock
ingredients.notes: ask your fishmonger for the real deal

ingredients.name: lacto-vegetarian saffron
ingredients.notes: use the real deal, not the powdered crap

instructions: heat the iron teppan over medium-high heat until it's screaming hot.
add the olive oil and swirl it around to coat the pan.
add the chopped onion and cook until it starts to caramelize, about 5 minutes.
add the minced garlic and cook for another minute, stirring constantly.
add the katsuramuki-cut okra and cook until it starts to soften, about 3 minutes.
add the smoked haddock and cook until it starts to flake apart, about 3-4 minutes.
add the aekjeot and cook for another minute, stirring constantly.
add the lacto-vegetarian saffron and cook for another minute, stirring constantly.
stir in the lemon juice and cook for another minute.
use a spatula to gently fold the ingredients together, being careful not to break up the fish.
cook for another 2-3 minutes, until the paella is golden and the fish is cooked through.
remove from heat and garnish with chopped fresh cilantro.
